title: Laboratory_Investigations_of_Low-Swirl_Injectors_Operating_With_Syngases
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 2]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content, as requested:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   `S = (2/3) * tan(alpha) * (1 - R^3) / sqrt(1 - R^2 + [m^2 * (1/R^2 - 1)^2] * R^2)`
*   `1 - (dU(x_f - x_0) / (U_0 * dx) )= (S_T / U_0) = (S_L / U_0) + K * (u'/ U_0)`

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 1: LSI prototype for Taurus 70 engine**
    * Components:
        * Center channel
        * Swirl annulus
    * Values:
        * 3.05 mm
        * 3.26 mm
        * 3.45 mm
        * LSI flame at STP & U0 = 9.6 m/s
        * Quartz cylinder (only used for measuring emissions)
*   **Fig. 2: Schematics of the LSI setup for atmospheric studies**
    *   Components:
        *   Venturi Premixer
        *   Fuel
        *   Air
        *   PIV seeds
        *   Flow Settling Chamber
        *   LSI swirler

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Swirl number (*S*): 0.4-0.55, 0.51
*   Vane angle (*alpha*): 40 deg
*   Ratio of unswirled to swirled mass fluxes (*m*): 0.39
*   Center-channel to injector radius ratio (*R*): 0.63
*   SoLoNOx swirler internal radius: 3.175 cm
*   LSI diameter: 6.35 cm
*   Center-channel diameter: 4 cm
*   Swirler recess: 9.5 cm
*   Settling Chamber diameter: 25.4 cm
*   Feed-tube length: 30 cm
*   Bulk flow velocity (*U*<sub>0</sub>): 0.2-0.3 m/s, 22 m/s
*   PIV laser pulses: 120 mJ at 532 nm
*   PIV camera resolution: 2048 x 2048 pixel
*   Field Of View: 13x13 cm^2
*   Pixel resolution: 0.065 mm/pixel
*   Air supply capacity: 65 g/s

I hope this is helpful!

title: Laboratory_Investigations_of_Low-Swirl_Injectors_Operating_With_Syngases
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's the breakdown of the technical content from the provided research page:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**
*   None explicitly presented.

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 3(a): Lean blow-off limits vs. bulk velocity - LBO**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: U0, m/s (Bulk velocity)
        *   y-axis: ΦLBO (Lean Blow-Off Limit vs. Equivalence Ratio)
    *   Curves:
        *   0.5H2/0.5CO: Triangle Marker Curve near 0.6 @ 20 m/s
        *   CH4: Square Marker Curve near 0.6 @ 20 m/s
        *   SG1 - 0.2H2/0.4CO/0.4CH4: Down Triangle Marker Curve near 0.35 @ 20 m/s
        *   SG2 - 0.3H2/0.3CO/0.4CO2: Circle Marker Curve near 0.3 @ 20 m/s.
        *   SG3 - 0.6H2/0.4CO: Inverted Triangle Marker Curve just below 0.3 @ 20 m/s
        *   H2: Diamond Marker Curve near 0.25 @ 20 m/s

*   **Fig. 3(b): Lean blow-off limits vs. bulk velocity - Temperature**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: U0, m/s (Bulk velocity)
        *   y-axis: Tad, K (Adiabatic Flame Temperature)
    *   Curves:
        *   0.5H2/0.5CO: Triangle Marker Curve at about 1600 K to 1800 K @ 20 m/s
        *   CH4: Square Marker Curve just below 1600 K @ 20 m/s
        *   SG1 - 0.2H2/0.4CO/0.4CH4: Down Triangle Marker Curve just above 1200 K @ 20 m/s
        *   SG2 - 0.3H2/0.3CO/0.4CO2: Circle Marker Curve just below 1200 K @ 20 m/s
        *   SG3 - 0.6H2/0.4CO: Inverted Triangle Marker Curve just under 1000 K @ 20 m/s
        *   H2: Diamond Marker Curve just above 600 K @ 20 m/s

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1: Syngas compositions and conditions for the PIV experiments**
    *   Headers: Name, Composition,  φ,  Tad (K),  S1 (m/s)
    *   Data:
        *   SG1: 0.2 H2–0.4 CO–0.4 CH4, 0.52, 1591, 0.14; 0.58, 1708, 0.18
        *   SG2: 0.3 H2–0.3 CO=0.4 CO2, 0.55, 1575, 0.14; 0.64, 1698, 0.22
        *   SG3: 0.6 H2–0.4 CO, 0.45, 1

title: Laboratory_Investigations_of_Low-Swirl_Injectors_Operating_With_Syngases
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here is the extracted technical content from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Δu = S_r * (τ - 1)
*   τ = T_ad / T_0
*   a_1 = δU/δx/U_0
*   q' = ((u'^2 +v'^2)^(1/2))/2

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 4(a):**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: NOx, ppm (@15% O2)
        *   x-axis: Tad, K
    *   Curves:
        *   Differents syngases.
    *   Key values:
        *   NG T = 671K, P = 12 atm
        *   NG T = 720K, P = 15 atm
            *   CH4, STP
            *   C2H4, STP
            *   H2, STP
            *   0.6 CH4 /0.4 H2, STP
            *   0.5 CH4 /0.5 CO, STP
            *   0.2 H2 /0.4 CO /0.4 CH4, STP
            *   0.3 H2 /0.3 CO /0.4 CO2, STP
            *   0.6 H2 /0.4 CO, STP
            *   0.5 H2 /0.5 CO, STP
*   **Figure 4(b):**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: CO ppm (@15% O2)
        *   x-axis: Tad K
    *   Curves:
        *   Differents syngases.
    *   Key values:
        *   NG T = 671K, P = 12 atm
        *   NG T = 720K, P = 15 atm
            *   CH4, STP
            *   C2H4, STP
            *   H2, STP
            *   0.6 CH4 /0.4 H2, STP
            *   0.5 CH4 /0.5 CO, STP
            *   0.2 H2 /0.4 CO /0.4 CH4, STP
            *   0.3 H2 /0.3 CO /0.4 CO2, STP
            *   0.6 H2 /0.4 CO, STP
            *   0.5 H2 /0.5 CO, STP

*   **Figure 5:**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: x/D
        *   x-axis: r/D
        *   Legends: uv / u'v'
    *   Curves:
        *   Vectors showing velocity and turbulence.
    *   Key values:
        *   SG1 - 0.2 H2 / 0.4 CO / 0.4 CH4
        *   SG2 - 0.3 H2 / 0.3 CO / 0.4 CO2
        *   SG3 - 0.6 H2 / 0.4 CO
        *    U0=14 m/s
        *   Tad ≈1700 K

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 5:** Normalized velocity vectors with different fuel compositions:
    *   Vectors representing normalized velocity.
    *   Top continuous lines mark boundaries of the recirculation bubble.

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 6(a):**
    *   Axes: x (mm), U/U0
    *   Curves: SG1 U = 10 m/s, SG1 U = 14 m/s, SG1 U = 18 m/s; SG2 U= 10 m/s, SG2 U= 14 m/s, SG2 U= 18 m/s; SG3 U = 10 m/s, SG3 U= 14 m/s, SG3 U= 18 m/s; U/U0 ranges from 0 to 1

*   **Fig. 6(b):**
    *   Axes: x (mm), q'/U0
    *   Curves: SG1, SG2, SG3 (different velocities); q'/U0 ranges from 0 to 0.15

*   **Fig. 7(a):**
    *   Axes: r (mm), U/U0
    *   Curves: SG1, SG2, SG3 (different velocities); U/U0 ranges from 0 to 1

*   **Fig. 7(b):**
    *   Axes: r (mm), V/U0
    *   Curves: SG1, SG2, SG3 (different velocities); V/U0 from -0.5 to 0.5

*   **Fig. 7(c):**
    *   Axes: r (mm), v/U0
    *   Curves: SG1, SG2, SG3 (different velocities); v/U0 ranges from 0 to 0.2

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Tad ≈ 1600 K (adiabatic flame temperature approximately 1600 Kelvin)
*   ar = δV/δr/U0 ≈ 0.012 mm^-1 (normalized radial divergence rates) (delta V / delta r /U0
*   7 m/s < U < 22 m/s (velocity range)
*   5.08 cm ID LSBs (low-swirl burners inner diameter)
*   R = 0.8 and 0.6 (presumably swirl number)
*   K = 1.73 (correlation constant for methane/air flames)
*   K = 2.14 (correlation constant including all low-swirl burners)
*   K = 3.15 (correlation constant for H2 flames)

title: Laboratory_Investigations_of_Low-Swirl_Injectors_Operating_With_Syngases
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's the breakdown of the technical content from the image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   S_t/S_L = 1 + 3.15 u'/S_L (Correlation for H2)
*   S_t/S_L = 1 + 1.73 u'/S_L (Correlation for CH4)

**2. Graphs:**

*   **Fig. 8:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: u'/S_L (unitless, likely non-dimensionalized turbulent velocity)
        *   y-axis: S_t/S_L (unitless, ratio of turbulent flame speed to laminar flame speed)
    *   Curves:
        *   Correlation for H2: S_t/S_L = 1 + 3.15 u'/S_L
        *   Correlation for CH4: S_t/S_L = 1 + 1.73 u'/S_L
    *   Data:
        *   Data from H2/CO, H2/N2, CH4/N2, CH4/CO2, CH4/H2, H2/CH4 blends
        *   Data from SG1, SG2, SG3 flames
*   **Fig. 10:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: % H2
        *   y-axis: phi (Equivalence ratio)
    *   Curves: None
    *   Data: Equivalence ratios for high-pressure experiments, ranging from 0.3 to 0.55

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 9:** Syngas composition triangle.
    *   Components at corners: H2, CO, CH4
    *   Data are shown as circular dots

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Nozzle exit velocity: 20-40 m/s
*   Temperature range: 450 K < T < 505 K
*   Pressure range: 1.5 atm < P < 14 atm
*   Pressure: P = 8 atm
*   Camcorder: 30 fps
*   Equivalence ratio: φ = 0.28
*   H2 concentration: 20% < H2 < 56%
*   CO concentration: 25% < CO < 60%
*   CH4 concentration: 19% < CH4 < 51%
*   Equivalence ratio range: 0.3 to 0.55
*   Adiabatic flame temperature range: 1350 K < T_ad < 1800 K
*   Optimum operating range restriction: U_0 < 20 m/s

title: Laboratory_Investigations_of_Low-Swirl_Injectors_Operating_With_Syngases
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 7]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content from the provided image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   ax = dU/dx/Uo (1/mm)
*   ar = dV/dr/Uo (1/mm)
*   m = mc/ms
*   q' = (u'^2 + v'^2)1/2
*   Re = 2RiUo/v
*   R = Rc/Ri

**2. Graphs/Charts:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Flame Images (Fig. 11):**
    *   Flow direction indicated by an arrow.
    *   Length: 7.5 cm
    *   (a) 8% H2, 61% CO, 31% CH4, Φ = 0.55
        *   T = 444 K, P = 7.9 atm, Uo = 17.0 m/s
    *   (b) 20% H2, 40% CO, 40% CH4, Φ = 0.28
        *   T = 509 K, P = 7.8 atm, Uo = 31.8 m/s
    *   (c) 15% H2, 68% CO, 17% CH4, Φ = 0.41
        *   T = 442 K, P = 7.87 atm, Uo = 16.87 m/s
    *   (d) 51% H2, 26% CO, 23% CH4, Φ = 0.39
        *   T = 442 K, P = 7.87 atm, Uo = 16.65 m/s

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Full size LSI: 6.35 cm
*   Reduced-scale LSI: 2.54 cm
*   Temperature Range: 450 K < T < 505 K
*   Pressure: 8 atm

**Nomenclature (List of Variables and Definitions):**

*   ax = normalized axial flow divergence (1/mm)
*   ar = normalized radial flow divergence (1/mm)
*   Li = swirler recess distance
*   m = mc/ms = mass flux ratio
*   mc = mass flux through center channel
*   ms = mass flux through swirl annulus
*   q' = 2D turbulent kinetic energy= (u'^2 + v'^2)^1/2
*   Re = Reynolds number 2RiUo/v
*   R = Rc/Ri = ratio of the center channel radius Rc to injector radius Ri
*   R = radial distance
*   S = swirl number
*   Sl = laminar flame speed
*   St = turbulent flame speed
*   Tad = adiabatic flame temperature
*   Uo = bulk flow velocity
*   U = axial velocity
*   u' = axial rms velocity
*   v' = radial rms velocity
*   uv = shear stress
*   x = axial distance from injector exit
*   xf = leading edge position of the flame brush
*   xo = virtual origin of divergent flow
